commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the top-level EmbeddedTuiBackend import from src/tui/tui.ts and dynamically imports it only when runTui enters local embedded mode.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main statically imports embedded-backend from the TUI module, and embedded-backend imports the gateway-side modules named in the PR. I did not run an end-to-end timing reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed: the patch has no discrete actionable defect from review, and the remaining action is maintainer review/landing coordination for a protected stacked PR.

target.title: perf(tui): defer EmbeddedTuiBackend import until local mode
target.body:
Stacked on #84686.

## Summary

`src/tui/tui.ts:31` static-imports `EmbeddedTuiBackend`, which transitively pulls gateway server-side modules (`server-model-catalog`, `session-reset-service`, `sessions-patch`, `gateway-chat-display-projection`, `cli-session-history`, `live-chat-projector`, etc.) into every TUI process at module-eval time. The remote-mode TUI never constructs an embedded backend — it's a thin WebSocket client.

This PR gates the import behind `opts.local` via `await import()`. In remote mode the embedded-backend module (and its gateway-side fanout) is never loaded.

Follow-up to the comment thread on #84686: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84686#issuecomment-4502318462

## Mode behavior

- **Remote TUI (`openclaw tui`)**: `embedded-backend.js` is never imported. Gateway-side modules it pulled in (model catalog, session reset, sessions patch, etc.) drop off the remote TUI module graph entirely.
- **Embedded TUI (`openclaw tui --local`, `chat`, `terminal`, callers that pass `opts.backend`)**: unchanged. `EmbeddedTuiBackend` is dynamically imported and instantiated as before.

## Verification

- `pnpm tsgo:core --noEmit` clean
- `pnpm build` clean (no `INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT` warning)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts src/agents/context.lookup.test.ts src/config/io.meta.test.ts src/config/runtime-snapshot.test.ts` — 102/102 pass
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/tui/tui.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `pnpm format:check src/tui/tui.ts` — clean

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: remote-mode `openclaw tui` evaluates gateway server-side modules at startup via the static `EmbeddedTuiBackend` import, even though they're never called.
- Real environment tested: verified statically that `embedded-backend.js` has no other prod static importer (`grep -rn embedded-backend src --include='*.ts' | grep -v test` returns only the new dynamic site). Build emits no ineffective-dynamic-import warning.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm tsgo:core --noEmit`; `pnpm build`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts src/agents/context.lookup.test.ts src/config/io.meta.test.ts src/config/runtime-snapshot.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: typecheck clean; build clean (no warnings); 102 targeted tests pass; lint and format clean.
- Observed result after fix: remote TUI module graph no longer references `embedded-backend.js` or its gateway-side fanout; local TUI behavior unchanged (tests cover both paths).
- What was not tested: end-to-end manual cold-start timing for `openclaw tui` against a warm gateway; `--local` mode regression check (`chat`/`terminal` cold-start path).
target.author: romneyda
